title: Validate Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-MXFP4 at TP=4 (with allreduce-fusion workaround)
description: Serve the large model at /data/models/amd-Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-MXFP4 at TP=4 via sgl_runner. Use TOKENSPEED_DISABLE_ALLREDUCE_FUSION=1 (issue #2 workaround). Confirm coherence; characterize the async wrong-first-token+hang from issue #1.
notes: ROOT-CAUSE MATRIX (397B TP=4, offline repro /tmp/repro_issue1.py, prompt 'The capital of France is' greedy): [fusion ON, overlap ON]=BROKEN first_tok 947 ' sp'; [fusion ON, overlap OFF]=CORRECT first_tok 11751 ' Paris'; [fusion OFF(=issue#2 workaround), overlap OFF]=BROKEN ' |'; [fusion OFF, overlap ON]=BROKEN. => (1) issue#1 confirmed in overlap loop (disable_overlap_schedule=True fixes it). (2) NEW: TOKENSPEED_DISABLE_ALLREDUCE_FUSION=1 itself corrupts TP=4 (non-fused allreduce+rmsnorm broken) independent of overlap.
close_reason: TP=4 validated/characterized. Correct config at TP=4 = allreduce fusion ON + overlap disabled (--enforce-eager or disable_overlap_schedule). The issue#2 workaround DISABLE_ALLREDUCE_FUSION=1 must NOT be used at TP=4 (it corrupts independently). Confirmed issue#1 lives in the overlap scheduler stream handoff. Handing to tokenspeed-do3.

title: [issue#1 P0] Qwen3.5-397B async kernel-ordering race: wrong first token + decode hang
description: github raikonenfnu/tokenspeed#1. On 397B TP=4/8, async execution produces wrong first token (4262 'PP' instead of 11751 ' Paris') then hangs in decode at reset_states (runtime_states.py:79). Disappears under HIP_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1 --enforce-eager => stream-ordering/sync bug. Localize via selective sync, add record_stream/event so reset_states reuse waits for prior step consumers. Audit runtime/engine/event_loop.py (_dispatch_forward l.630, _commit_forward_results l.1071), runtime/states/runtime_states.py:79, model_executor.py:1223.
notes: DISCRIMINATOR (397B TP=4, fusion ON): overlap ON + graphs = BROKEN (' sp'); overlap ON + EAGER = BROKEN (' r'); overlap OFF + graphs = CORRECT (' Paris'). => bug is in the OVERLAP SCHEDULER STREAM LOGIC, not CUDA-graph replay. enforce_eager alone does NOT fix (issue's HIP_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1 masked it by serializing both streams). Only manifests with Iris fused allreduce_residual_rmsnorm (triton_allreduce_residual_rmsnorm on AMD via comm_manager input_reduce_norm/post_attn_reduce_norm). Hypothesis: Iris symmetric-heap workspace/signal flags reused across consecutive overlapped forward steps without an event guarding the execution_stream<->default_stream handoff.
close_reason: FIXED. Added trailing device_barrier() in IrisAllReduceResidualRMSNorm.fused() (tokenspeed-kernel ops/communication/iris.py) to close the read-after-write hazard on the shared symmetric-heap _input_buf. Validated at TP=4 default config (overlap ON + allreduce fusion ON + CUDA graphs): full 6-prompt coherence_suite now fully coherent ('The capital of France is Paris.', 17x4=68, primes 2/3/5/7/11), vs catastrophic CJK/empty garbage before. enforce_eager was insufficient (bug was in overlap stream skew, not graphs); disabling overlap was the prior workaround.

title: Validate Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-MXFP4 coherence (TP=1)
description: After applying the 3 fix commits, serve the small model at /data/models/amd-Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-MXFP4 via sgl_runner/amd-Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-MXFP4/run_tokenspeed.sh (TP=1) and confirm coherent greedy output via test_coherence.sh / coherence_suite.py. This is the fast bringup gate before touching 397B.
close_reason: 35B-A3B-MXFP4 TP=1 offline coherence PASS with all 3 fixes applied. Greedy outputs fully coherent: 'The capital of France is Paris.', correct primary colors, square lambda, 17*4=68, Rayleigh scattering, first 5 primes. No weight-load crash (GDN bf16 fix), no token corruption (silu MoE fix), no h-track NotImplementedError. Model load+capture+6 gens in ~95s on 1x MI350X.

title: Apply GDN/MoE/quant fix commits (cherry-pick 3 commits)
description: Cherry-pick cf5e5d4 (keep Qwen3.5 GDN in_proj/out_proj bf16 under MXFP4), f3ba2c2 (stop using gfx950 Gluon MXFP4 MoE for plain-silu models), 5db9e3f (drop prefix-cache h-track on portable Triton extend path) from raikonen/gdn_fixes onto raikonen/gdn_rebased. Drop the .beads/config.yaml and .beads/issues.jsonl hunks from cf5e5d4 since beads was just re-initialized.
close_reason: Cherry-picked cf5e5d4, f3ba2c2, 5db9e3f onto raikonen/gdn_rebased (commits 54ef3bd, 74213a2, a7f9f65). Dropped the stale .beads hunks from cf5e5d4; verified each applied patch body is identical to the original (only commit-hash header and a benign hunk line-number offset differ).

title: [issue#2 P1] Qwen3.5-397B TP=8 Iris symmetric-heap fd-passing deadlock
description: github raikonenfnu/tokenspeed#2. At TP=8, fused allreduce+RMSNorm lazily builds Iris symmetric-heap context -> all_gather_object hangs on all 8 ranks (setup_fd_infrastructure). TP=4 works. Likely fix: pass explicit device_id=torch.device('cuda', local_rank) to init_process_group, or dedicated gloo PG for fd exchange, or eager Iris init. Workaround in place: TOKENSPEED_DISABLE_ALLREDUCE_FUSION=1. Code: tokenspeed-kernel ops/communication/iris.py (l.91, l.702), ops/communication/triton.py:1568, runtime/layers/layernorm.py:309, runtime/distributed/comm_manager.py:306.
close_reason: FIXED via explicit device_id. process_group_manager.init_distributed now passes device_id=torch.device(config.device, config.gpu_id) to dist.init_process_group (distributed_initializer already set_device per rank). Eliminates the NCCL 'Guessing device ID' path that deadlocked all_gather_object in Iris fd-passing at TP=8. Validated at TP=8 (default config, allreduce fusion ON, overlap ON): reaches first forward + serves coherent output ('The capital of France is Paris.', primes 2/3/5/7/11); warning gone; 8 Iris ranks init OK. Combined with issue#1 barrier fix, TP=8 no longer needs TOKENSPEED_DISABLE_ALLREDUCE_FUSION.
